evidence.authority: National Institute of Standards and Technology, Applied and Computational Mathematics Division
evidence.excerpt: The SLATEC Library is a public-domain Fortran library.
evidence.scope: SLATEC Common Mathematical Library, Version 4.1 (July 1993); not an inference merely from NIST association.
evidence.statement: NIST expressly describes the SLATEC Library, Version 4.1, as public domain and identifies Netlib as its whole-or-part distribution channel.

evidence.authority: National Technical Information Service, U.S. Department of Commerce
evidence.excerpt: SLATEC is a public domain library of over 1000 mathematical routines.
evidence.scope: SLATEC routines described by the 1987 SLADOC technical report; corroborating institutional evidence, not a licence inferred from sponsorship.
evidence.statement: The authenticated NTIS record independently describes the SLATEC routine library as public domain and names included packages including LINPACK, EISPACK, DEPAC, FISHPACK, and QUADPACK.

evidence.authority: Netlib SLATEC distribution index
evidence.excerpt: FNLIB subset of SLATEC; main SLATEC Fortran source.
evidence.scope: Only the selected source URLs under https://www.netlib.org/slatec/fnlib/ and https://www.netlib.org/slatec/src/.
evidence.statement: The Version 4.1 Netlib index identifies the selected fnlib and src units as subsets of the SLATEC Common Mathematical Library, rather than unrelated Netlib-hosted code.

records.governing_notice: NIST and NTIS expressly identify SLATEC as public domain.
records.redistribution_conditions: No licence-imposed condition is stated in the reviewed public-domain records; preserve factual source attribution.
records.third_party_incorporation_notes: The fnlib subset is historical source provenance only. It is not treated as a grant covering incorporated third-party routines.

evidence.scope: SLATEC routines described by the 1987 SLADOC technical report; corroborating institutional evidence, not a licence inferred from sponsorship.
evidence.statement: The authenticated NTIS record independently describes the SLATEC routine library as public domain and names included packages including LINPACK, EISPACK, DEPAC, FISHPACK, and QUADPACK.

evidence.authority: Netlib SLATEC distribution index
evidence.excerpt: FNLIB subset of SLATEC; main SLATEC Fortran source.
evidence.scope: Only the selected source URLs under https://www.netlib.org/slatec/fnlib/ and https://www.netlib.org/slatec/src/.
evidence.statement: The Version 4.1 Netlib index identifies the selected fnlib and src units as subsets of the SLATEC Common Mathematical Library, rather than unrelated Netlib-hosted code.

records.governing_notice: NIST and NTIS expressly identify SLATEC as public domain.
records.redistribution_conditions: No licence-imposed condition is stated in the reviewed public-domain records; preserve factual source attribution.
records.third_party_incorporation_notes: The fnlib subset is historical source provenance only. It is not treated as a grant covering incorporated third-party routines.

evidence.authority: National Institute of Standards and Technology, Applied and Computational Mathematics Division
evidence.excerpt: The SLATEC Library is a public-domain Fortran library.
evidence.scope: SLATEC Common Mathematical Library, Version 4.1 (July 1993); not an inference merely from NIST association.
evidence.statement: NIST expressly describes the SLATEC Library, Version 4.1, as public domain and identifies Netlib as its whole-or-part distribution channel.
